Reason Given: AI-Driven Restructuring: Zuckerberg noted the need to become more capital efficient and move resources to the "AI discovery engine" and advertising platforms.

Job Position/Category: Mixed — recruiting, corporate, engineering, experimental projects (~6%)
Reason Given: CEO Sundar Pichai memo: company facing 'a different economic reality,' doubling down on AI while cutting staff in experimental projects. Redirected resources toward AI launch.
Part of cost-cutting plan saving $860M. Layoffs tied to macro conditions and restructuring, with some AI/automation efficiency cited.
AI-First Reallocation: Zuckerberg confirmed these cuts were necessary to refocus the technical workforce on "AI discovery engines" and advanced infrastructure.

CEO Jonah Peretti announced AI pivot in Jan 2023 memo: 'AI inspired content will move from R&D to core business.' April cuts shuttered BuzzFeed News. Peretti later said AI will 'replace the majority of static content.'
CEO Drew Houston cited AI era: 'The AI era of computing has finally arrived... our next stage of growth requires a different mix of skill sets, particularly in AI.'

*HIRING FREEZE — Economic impact, not direct layoffs. CEO Arvind Krishna: 'I could easily see 30% of that getting replaced by AI and automation over a five-year period.' Paused hiring for ~26,000 non-customer-facing roles. AskHR chatbot now handles 94% of routine HR queries without human handoff. Freeze active since May 2023.

Finalizing the AI Pivot: This was the final wave of the 10k plan. Zuckerberg confirmed the "Year of Efficiency" was designed to shift the "single largest investment" into advancing AI across all products.
CEO cited AI disruption: students turning to ChatGPT instead of Chegg's homework help platform. SEC filing stated cuts to 'better align the Company with its AI strategy.'
First redundancy plan in France explicitly caused by AI. 209 of 383 positions made redundant to be replaced by AI-powered systems.
Part of company 'reinvention' restructuring. Automation reshaping print and services businesses; cuts across all levels and areas.
Focused more heavily on AI-assisted creation tools for developers. Cuts amid broader strategic changes and revenue challenges.
Spokesperson stated cuts could be 'attributed to AI.' Company shifted to AI-driven content creation. CEO Luis von Ahn later confirmed replacing contractors with AI wherever feasible.

CEO Luis von Ahn company-wide email: 'gradually stop using contractors to do work that AI can handle.' Declared 'AI-first' company. 'New hires will only be authorized if a team is unable to automate.'
SVP Philipp Schindler memo: restructuring ad sales amid AI integration. 'We map customers to the right specialist teams.' Coincided with broader AI restructuring of Google Assistant.

Arena Group lost SI license after missed $3.75M payment; previously caught publishing AI-generated articles with fake AI-generated author photos. Layoffs driven by licensing loss, but AI content controversy contributed to brand damage.
SAP announced €2B restructuring: 'In 2024, SAP will further increase its focus on key strategic growth areas, in particular Business AI.' Most positions covered by voluntary leave and reskilling.

Job Position/Category: Fraud detection, risk management, customer support (~9%)
Reason Given: CEO Alex Chriss: 'drive more focus and efficiency, deploy automation, and consolidate our technology.' Automation expanded across key functions.
AI and machine learning are being used to automate tasks previously handled by managers; jobs are considered "redundant" and will not return.
column7: CEO Carol Tomé: cutting 12,000 jobs to save $1B. 'We are going to fit our organization to our strategy and align resources against what's wildly important.' AI cited as enabling management efficiencies.

Reason Given: Business restructuring focused on AI-enabled writing and productivity tools. Aligned teams and resources with strategic priorities around AI.
AI-assisted workflows across tax and finance products enabling reallocation toward critical strategic areas.
Reorganized around demand for AI servers and infrastructure. Automation reduced need for some traditional roles in sales and operations.

Job Position/Category: Mixed — via hiring freeze and attrition
Reason Given: CEO Siemiatkowski: workforce dropped from 5,000 to 3,800 (24% in one year) through hiring freeze and natural attrition enabled by AI. AI chatbot doing work of 700 agents. Plans to reach 2,000 employees.
CEO Drew Houston: trimming bloated areas, building leaner team with bigger focus on AI. Refocused around AI-powered search/productivity tools.
CEO Carl Eschenbach: 'Companies everywhere are reimagining how work gets done, and the increasing demand for AI has the potential to drive a new era of growth.' Cuts to prioritize AI investment.
"Network Reconfiguration" utilizing increased technology and automation to streamline operations as the company trims Amazon volumes.
CEO George Kurtz memo: 'We're operating in a market and technology inflection point, with AI reshaping every industry, accelerating threats, and evolving customer needs.'
CEO cited 'new realities of AI'; students increasingly using ChatGPT over Chegg. Google AI Overviews restricting web traffic. Filed lawsuit against Google.
CEO Satya Nadella memo: company must 'imagine its mission for a new era.' ~30% of code now written by AI. Majority of cuts were programmers. Shifted resources toward AI products.
CEO: aim to 'simplify hiring using AI and technology and data to reduce manual work.' AI changing job search industry.
Redirected capital toward AI chips and advanced manufacturing. Automation and AI-driven efficiencies reduced workforce needs.
Cut 2% of workforce; responded to shifting demand and influence of automation and AI on IT roles.
Shifting focus towards a greater use of AI in content moderation.
Shifting focus toward AI-driven networking and security products. CFO: 'need to pivot more resources into the fastest-growth areas.'
CEO Marc Benioff on podcast: 'I reduced it from 9,000 heads to about 5,000 because I need less heads.' AI agents handling ~50% of customer interactions.
CEO Micha Kaufman: cuts needed to become 'AI-first company.' AI described as 'coming for your job.' Will exclusively hire individuals proficient in AI.
Integrating automation and AI into processes; moving manual customer service tasks to automated systems.
Restructuring to align with AI push; part of $865M "business optimization" program.
column7: CEO Julie Sweet: 'Those we cannot reskill will be exited.' Restructuring tied to AI and automation. Simultaneously grew AI workforce from 40K to 77K.

Reason Given: CEO: 'new realities of AI' and diminished traffic from internet search. Declining revenue made cuts unavoidable. AI reshaped student behavior.
Eliminated design and UX roles; redirected resources toward AI engineering and infrastructure.
First mass layoff in company history. Staff told their roles had been replaced by AI-driven payroll and back-office systems.
SVP Beth Galetti: 'This generation of AI represents the most transformative technology we've encountered since the Internet.' CEO Jassy warned AI would reduce workforce.
AI-driven tools for pricing, scheduling, shipment tracking. Average headcount down 10.8% YoY. 'Decouple headcount growth from volume growth.'
Automated non-client-facing work with AI systems. Offered retraining in AI and data analytics.
CEO Arvind Krishna: AI agents replaced hundreds of back-office roles. Shifted talent toward AI and quantum computing. Layoffs a 'direct outcome of automation.'
AI increasingly replacing manual processes in travel insurance division.
Major layoffs following acquisition; generative AI as means of 'fresh agility and scale.'
Part of restructuring to improve digital business; AI-related digital transformation.

SEC filing stated cuts made 'to reduce operating expenses and optimize the organizational structure in support of long-term growth and in light of AI-driven efficiency improvements.' CEO Jeff Kip had indicated in August 2025 that Angi was investing in AI to facilitate quicker connections between homeowners and service providers. Angi launched AI Helper in June 2025. Expected annual savings of $70-80M.
Laid off 119 people in an AI-expansion move, per Folha de S. Paulo.
Shifting resources toward technology and data-driven systems. AI across investment analysis and operations reduced need for traditional support roles.
Strategic shifts, pivoting toward AI. CEO stated 'these changes are not driven by... an effort to replace people with AI' but redirecting spending to cloud/AI.

Job Position/Category: Supply chain, digital operations, tech division
Reason Given: AI-driven forecasting reduced staffing needs; automated parts of supply chain and digital operations.
Efficiency and AI transition: Leadership noted that projects once requiring large teams are being consolidated via AI automation and flatter organizational structures.
Efficiency-driven cuts; 'choosing to make these changes at a moment of strength.' Further 1,300 planned.
SVP Beth Galetti memo: 'This generation of AI is the most transformative technology we've seen since the Internet... We need to be organized more leanly, with fewer layers.'
Streamlining all end-to-end work processes using automation and AI.

Workforce reduced by roughly 30% since start of 2026 through multiple layoff rounds. Company deploying AI tools to increase productivity. Winklevoss-led exchange lost over $500M in 2025. As of March 1, company employed about 445 people.
AI-driven restructure in collaboration with India's Infosys.
Shift towards AI; cuts primarily affect support staff. Decision followed review of operations aimed at improving efficiency through AI.

Spokesperson: 'We are fundamentally reorganizing our internal operations to become an AI-native agentic organization.' And: 'We've integrated advanced AI agents and automation across our core functions -- Sales, Ops, Design, and Marketing. In many areas, tasks that were previously manual are now handled by intelligent systems.' Layoffs phased over six months as AI agents were tested and deployed. Co-founder Saurabh Jain also departed. Company insisted cuts were 'not a reactive cost-cut' but 'a strategic reallocation of resources.'
CEO Zubin Appoo: 'AI enables greater productivity in less time and with fewer employees.' Integrating AI into customer software and internal operations.
CEO Tim Steiner: 'We have largely completed a very significant phase of investment in our robotics and automation capabilities.'
CEO Jack Dorsey: 'Intelligence tools we're creating, paired with smaller and flatter teams, are enabling a new way of working which fundamentally changes what it means to build and run a company.'
CEO Mike Cannon-Brookes internal memo: restructuring to focus on AI and enterprise growth. Repositioning for the 'AI era.'

Job Position/Category: Recruiting, Sales, Operations, and Reality Labs
Reason Given: Funding AI Infrastructure: Part of a broader restructuring to offset multi-billion dollar investments in AI models (Llama) and data center projects.

New CEO Diran Li announced pivot to AI-first organization, cutting human roles across research, data, and content production. Former CEO Eric Turner confirmed numerous employees laid off as company intensifies focus on AI-driven products for institutional clients.
Roles eliminated as digital banking tools and AI reduced staffing in support functions.

CEO Kris Marszalek announced 12% layoff citing enterprise-wide AI integration: 'We are joining the list of companies integrating enterprise-wide AI. Companies that do not make this pivot will immediately fail.' Cuts targeted 'roles that do not adapt in our new world.'

Job Position/Category: Cloud / Revenue & Health Sciences / SaaS & Virtual Operations / NetSuite India / Engineering
Reason Given: Oracle began mass layoffs via 6 a.m. termination emails signed 'Oracle Leadership' with no prior notice from HR or managers. TD Cowen estimates 20,000-30,000 affected (~18% of workforce). Hardest-hit: Revenue & Health Sciences and SaaS operations (~30% each), plus NetSuite India Development Centre. Cuts expected to free up $8-10B in cash flow to fund $156B AI data center buildout. Oracle disclosed $2.1B restructuring plan in March 2026 SEC filing. Bloomberg reported March 5 that cuts would target 'roles the company expects it will need less of due to AI.' Oracle declined to comment.

Strategic shift toward AI: CEO Mark Zuckerberg stated 2026 is the year AI "dramatically changes how we work," emphasizing "AI-native tooling" to allow single individuals to do the work of large teams.
